task_completion.description: Verify that the manual threshold is set to the specified Hounsfield Unit (HU) range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the threshold configuration panel in the InVesalius GUI. The minimum threshold value should be set to 350 and the maximum threshold value should be set to 2000.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 3D surface generation settings match the requested parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 3D surface creation dialog or panel. The quality should be set to 'Optimal', and the options for 'Fill holes' and 'Keep largest region' must be checked/enabled.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 3D surface model of the cortical bone has been successfully generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 3D viewer pane in InVesalius. A 3D rendered model of a skull/bone should be visible, indicating the surface was generated from the mask.

task_completion.description: Verify that the InVesalius project was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/cortical_project.inv3', the InVesalius window title bar displaying 'cortical_project.inv3', or a file manager window showing the file in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 3D surface was exported as an STL file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Export 3D Surface' dialog saving to '/home/ga/Documents/cortical_bone.stl', or a file manager/terminal window showing 'cortical_bone.stl' present in the Documents directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the InVesalius graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through InVesalius menus, panels, and dialogs rather than just executing a pre-written script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the 3D model was actually computed and rendered by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main 3D viewport in InVesalius should display the generated bone structure. It should not be empty or showing a generic placeholder.

integrity.description: Verify that the output files were created via the application's export/save functions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use the InVesalius 'Save Project' and 'Export 3D Surface' GUI features. There should be no terminal commands like 'touch /home/ga/Documents/cortical_bone.stl' used to fake the file creation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target thresholds are exactly 350 HU (min) and 2000 HU (max). The surface must be generated with 'Optimal' quality, 'Fill holes' enabled, and 'Keep largest region' enabled. The project must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/cortical_project.inv3 and the STL exported to /home/ga/Documents/cortical_bone.stl. A valid STL for this task will typically be over 100KB in size (representing >2000 triangles).
